seoTitle: How to Email a Google Doc as an Attachment (2026)
metaDescription: Send a Google Doc as an email attachment in PDF or Word format directly from the File menu, without downloading it to your device first.
intro: You don't need to download a document before sending it to someone by email. This guide covers Google Docs' built-in Email this file feature, which lets you attach a converted PDF or Word file directly from the File menu, fill in recipient details, and send it without ever leaving the document.

steps.heading: Open the File menu
steps.body: With your document open, click File to access the sharing and distribution options.

steps.heading: Select Email this file
steps.body: Hover over the Email option and click Email this file to open a message composition overlay right in your browser.

steps.heading: Choose your attachment format
steps.body: Use the format dropdown to convert your document into a PDF, Microsoft Word (DOCX), or Rich Text file before sending.

steps.heading: Fill in recipient details
steps.body: Add recipient email addresses, write a clear subject line, and draft your message body.

steps.heading: Send yourself a copy
steps.body: Check the Send a copy to myself box to keep a backup of the sent attachment in your own inbox.

steps.heading: Dispatch and confirm delivery
steps.body: Click Send to deliver the attachment, then check your sent folder to confirm the transmission went through.

faq.question: Can I send a document as an attachment without downloading it first?
faq.answer: Yes, the Email this file feature converts and attaches your document directly from within Google Docs, no manual download needed.

faq.question: What formats can I choose when emailing a document?
faq.answer: You can attach your file as a PDF, Microsoft Word (DOCX), or Rich Text format, depending on what the recipient needs.

faq.question: Will the recipient be able to edit the file I send this way?
faq.answer: If you send it as a Word or Rich Text attachment, yes, they'll be able to edit it in a compatible application; a PDF is generally view-only.

faq.question: How do I keep a record of what I sent?
faq.answer: Check the Send a copy to myself option before sending so a copy lands in your own inbox for reference.
